## Reasoning + Response
We are asked for \(a(n)=\mathrm{lcm}(1,2,\dots,n)\) for \(n=21\) through \(30\).

Start from the known value:
\[
a(20)=\mathrm{lcm}(1,\dots,20)=232792560.
\]

Now update only when a new number contributes a higher prime power.

- \(21=3\cdot 7\), no new prime power beyond \(a(20)\), so
  \[
  a(21)=232792560.
  \]
- \(22=2\cdot 11\), no new prime power, so
  \[
  a(22)=232792560.
  \]
- \(23\) is prime and new, so multiply by \(23\):
  \[
  a(23)=232792560\cdot 23=5354228880.
  \]
- \(24=2^3\cdot 3\), already covered, so
  \[
  a(24)=5354228880.
  \]
- \(25=5^2\), new higher power of \(5\), so multiply by \(5\):
  \[
  a(25)=5354228880\cdot 5=26771144400.
  \]
- \(26=2\cdot 13\), already covered, so
  \[
  a(26)=26771144400.
  \]
- \(27=3^3\), new higher power of \(3\), so multiply by \(3\):
  \[
  a(27)=26771144400\cdot 3=80313433200.
  \]
- \(28=2^2\cdot 7\), already covered, so
  \[
  a(28)=80313433200.
  \]
- \(29\) is prime and new, so multiply by \(29\):
  \[
  a(29)=80313433200\cdot 29=2329089562800.
  \]
- \(30=2\cdot 3\cdot 5\), already covered, so
  \[
  a(30)=2329089562800.
  \]

Thus
\[
[232792560,232792560,5354228880,5354228880,26771144400,26771144400,80313433200,80313433200,2329089562800,2329089562800].
